places.name: Convento de San Francisco Ossuary
places.description: The mortal remains of an estimated 70,000 people lie in the basement of a monastery in Lima, Peru.

places.name: Mercado de Brujas
places.description: A market for witches is hidden within a train station.

places.name: The Room of 10,000 Ancient Skulls
places.description: Lima’s Museum of Anthropology is lined with thousands of elongated human craniums.

places.name: Pachacamac
places.description: The remains of what was the most important religious complex in coastal Peru for over a millennium.

places.name: Huaca Pucllana
places.description: This stone complex marks the urban center of a lost civilization.

places.name: Brain Museum
places.description: Tucked behind the Institute of Neurological Science down a decrepit street resides the largest display of human brains in Latin America.

places.name: Bosque el Olivar (Olive Grove Forest)
places.description: A pretty park of over 1,600 olive trees that grew from just three saplings brought over from Spain.

places.name: Bosque el Olivar (Olive Grove Forest)
places.description: A pretty park of over 1,600 olive trees that grew from just three saplings brought over from Spain.

places.name: El Cacaotal
places.description: Visit Lima’s one-stop-shop for sustainably sourced and produced Peruvian chocolate.

places.name: Expreso Virgen de Guadalupe
places.description: This antique rail car has been reborn as an urban cafe.
